title: Copy and Paste Objects and Text
breadcrumbs: Copy and Paste Objects and Text
text: Copy and Paste Objects and Text To copy and paste objects, properties and text in the Uniface IDE, right-click and choose the appropriate command from the context menu. To copy an object or text, right-click the object and then choose Copy in the context menu. To paste an object or text, right-click an object or an area where the object should be pasted and then click Paste , or choose another Paste option (such as Paste as Child , Paste Before , or Paste After ). If the copied object is not valid in the target context, it cannot be pasted (and the Paste option(s) are not available in the context menu). When you copy a Uniface object such as a modeled field, and paste it into a code editor or another text editor, the fully-qualified name of the object is copied. When you copy a Snippet from the Resource Browser into the Script Editor or Layout Editor, the content of the Snippet is pasted. In the Script Editor and Layout Editor, you can use conventional keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V). This is not possible in other locations. You can also insert or copy/paste fields in the Structure pane, but not in the Canvas pane. You can insert or copy/paste multiple fields in one attempt in the Structure pane. Was this topic helpful?
